prompt: Ultra-realistic cinematic wide establishing shot inside a large, dark security operations command center (SOC) at night — a professional cyber-monitoring control room, like a NASA mission control or a corporate cyber-defense war room. The camera looks from the back of the room toward a massive curved video wall spanning the far wall, made of many tiled screens showing dark-themed security dashboards: a glowing world network map, live traffic graphs, streaming log panels, and telemetry — all in cool cyan on near-black. Dominating the center of the video wall, one large panel has just lit up with a prominent alert: a bold red-amber 'ALERT' banner, and a highlighted detection card reading the filename 'Q3_performance_report_2024.xlsx' with a destination label 'External AI service' and a small file-type tag and timestamp — the red-amber alert clearly standing out against the otherwise cyan wall. In the mid-ground, a few security analysts sit at tiered console desks facing the wall, seen only from behind as anonymous dark silhouettes rimmed by the screen glow, calm and watchful, no faces visible. The video wall is the key light source, casting cyan and alert-red glow across the dark room, silhouetting the operators and their desks, faint reflections on the polished floor. The room is dim but readable — deep controlled shadows with retained detail, a tidy high-end control-room environment with subtle ambient accent lighting along the floor and ceiling. Cohesive dark-tech color grade, cyan and controlled alert-red dominant, gentle contrast, no crushed blacks. Strong symmetrical composition with the video wall centered, calm darker negative space along the top of the frame for later headline text. Real fine cinematic grain, atmospheric haze catching the screen light, subtle depth. 24mm lens, f/2.8, ISO 800, deep-ish focus with the video wall sharp and the foreground silhouettes softly falling off. Documentary realism, premium dark-tech product-film aesthetic in the style of an Apple or Nvidia launch film, tense and composed. Keep all on-screen text short, crisp and perfectly legible; exactly spell 'Q3_performance_report_2024.xlsx' and 'ALERT'; do not garble characters. Keep people as distant anonymous silhouettes from behind, no visible faces. Balanced, legible exposure — bright, clearly exposed screen elements against controlled shadows with retained detail. No muddy, murky, or underexposed haze; no crushed midtones.
negative_prompt: gate, metal gate, barrier, BLOCKED sign, sealed portal, security checkpoint arch, warm cream UI, coral upload interface, single small monitor, empty room with no screens, visible faces, front-facing people, facing camera, deformed faces, distorted bodies, extra limbs, extra fingers, bright evenly-lit room, flat lighting, cartoon, CGI, illustration, 3d render look, plastic skin, oversaturated colors, neon overload, cluttered messy room, distorted screen text, gibberish text, garbled letters, misspelled words, warped monitors, watermark, logo watermark, low resolution, HDR halos, lens flare spam, underexposed, muddy, murky, crushed blacks, invisible details, washed out

settings.style: documentary realism, dark-tech cinematic, SOC alert dashboard, bookend of KF0
settings.lighting: monitor key light, cold cyan + alert-red glow, dim readable room, silhouette rim light
settings.camera_angle: over-the-shoulder of security analyst, monitor centered as focal point
settings.depth_of_field: shallow, silhouette soft, dashboard tack sharp
settings.quality: high detail, legible UI text, real interior grain

_note: KF6 (option B) — proxy DETECTS + ALERTS the company; company decides. Replaces deprecated gate-slam 'blocked' version. Bookends KF0 (employee monitor -> security team monitor). Headline: 'We see it. You decide.'
